segments.text: First listen, and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What do adolescents respect in parents?

translations.zh-Hans: 青少年尊重父母的什么品质？

segments.text: Parents are often upset when their children praise the homes of their friends and regard it as a slur on their own cooking or cleaning or furniture, and often are foolish enough to let the adolescents see that they are annoyed.

translations.zh-Hans: 孩子赞美朋友的家时，父母常感到不快，把这视为对自家饭菜、清洁或家具的贬低；他们还常愚蠢到让青少年看出自己的恼怒。

segments.text: They may even accuse them of disloyalty or make some spiteful remark about the friend's parents.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们甚至可能指责孩子不忠，或恶意评论朋友的父母。

segments.text: Such a loss of dignity and dissent into childish behavior on the part of the adults deeply shocks the adolescents and makes them resolve that in future they will not talk to their parents about the places or people they visit.

translations.zh-Hans: 成年人如此丧失尊严、堕入幼稚行为，会深深震惊青少年，使他们下定决心，以后不再和父母谈起自己去过的地方或见过的人。

segments.text: Before very long, the parents will be complaining that the child is so secretive and never tells them anything, but they seldom realize that they have brought this on themselves.

translations.zh-Hans: 不久父母便会抱怨孩子什么都藏在心里，从不告诉他们任何事，却很少意识到这是自己造成的。

segments.text: Disillusionment with the parents, however good and adequate they may be, both as parents and as individuals, is, to some degree, inevitable.

translations.zh-Hans: 无论父母作为父母或个人多么优秀称职，孩子对他们幻想破灭在某种程度上都不可避免。

segments.text: Most children have such a high ideal of their parents, unless the parents themselves have been unsatisfactory, that it can hardly hope to stand up to a realistic evaluation.

translations.zh-Hans: 除非父母本身很不称职，大多数孩子都对父母抱有极高理想，而这种理想几乎不可能经受现实评价。

segments.text: Parents would be greatly surprised and deeply touched if they realized how much belief their children usually have in their character and infallibility, and how much this faith means to a child.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果父母意识到孩子通常多么相信他们的品格和永不犯错，也明白这种信念对孩子多么重要，他们会既惊讶又感动。

segments.text: If parents were prepared for this adolescent reaction and realized that it was a sign that the child was growing up and developing valuable powers of observation and independent judgment, they would not be so hurt and therefore would

translations.zh-Hans: 如果父母对青少年的这种反应有所准备，并认识到它表明孩子正在长大，正在培养宝贵的观察力和独立判断力，他们就不会如此受伤，因而也

segments.text: not drive the child into opposition by resenting and resisting it.

translations.zh-Hans: 不会因怨恨和抵制而把孩子逼到对立面。

segments.text: The adolescent, with his passion for sincerity, always respects a parent who admits that he is wrong, or ignorant, or even that he has been unfair or unjust.

translations.zh-Hans: 青少年热爱真诚，总会尊重能够承认自己错误、无知，甚至承认自己曾经不公平或不公正的父母。

segments.text: What the child cannot forgive is the parent's refusal to admit these charges if the child knows them to be true.

translations.zh-Hans: 孩子无法原谅的是，明明知道指责属实，父母却拒不承认。

segments.text: Victorian parents believed that they kept their dignity by retreating behind an unreasoning, authoritarian attitude.

translations.zh-Hans: 维多利亚时代的父母认为，退守到不讲道理的权威姿态后面就能维护尊严。

segments.text: In fact, they did nothing of the kind, but children were then too cowed to let them know how they really felt.

translations.zh-Hans: 实际上他们根本没有做到，只是当时的孩子被吓得不敢让父母知道真实感受。

segments.text: Today, we tend to go to the other extreme, but on the whole this is a healthier attitude, both for the child and the parent.

translations.zh-Hans: 今天我们往往走向另一个极端，但总的说来，这对孩子和父母都是更健康的态度。

segments.text: It is always wiser and safer to face up to reality, however painful it may be at the moment.

translations.zh-Hans: 无论现实当下多么痛苦，正视它总是更明智、更安全。
